title: bromus arvensis subsp. patulus
intro: Bromus arvensis subsp. patulus, commonly known as ripgut brome, is a perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and fungal infections. The health benefits of ripgut brome include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural antiseptic, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Ripgut brome can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
